rag_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referring to component ports
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model for AGREE expressions
Write input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees when concrete bounds are provided
Relate outputs to inputs in component type guarantees, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths
generic_rules: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `guarantee` for property guarantees
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
